exemplar.title: Modelling the Volume of a Flower Vase through Integration

detailedExemplarData.title: Modelling the Volume of a Flower Vase through Integration

subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Clear logical sequence with introduction, aims, method, modelling, calculation, and conclusion
Well-organized headings and sub-headings facilitating reader navigation
weaknesses: Occasional abrupt omissions of algebraic working interrupt the flow
Background description is overly verbose with some unnecessary repetition
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is coherent and shows clear logical development, with a recognisable introduction-body-conclusion structure and effective use of headings, figures and tables (Presentation band 3). Minor organisational issues (e.g. figures placed far from commentary, verbose digressions and incomplete sentences) prevent it from reaching the highest band.

strengths: Effective use of labelled photographs, Desmos screenshots and algebraic notation to support understanding
Appropriate use of symbols such as Ï, â and definite integrals for most steps
weaknesses: Inconsistent figure numbering and citation style cause reader confusion
Omission of the differential term in one integral and lack of explicit parameter values reduce precision
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical communication is generally clear and mostly consistent, employing multiple representations (band 3 descriptors). However, inconsistent formatting (figure/citation mismatches), missing notation elements and occasional density in explanation limit the overall clarity and accuracy, placing it in band 2.

strengths: Practical water-test validation demonstrates initiative and connects theory to real-world application
Clear personal aims (function fitting, error testing) drive the exploration
weaknesses: Lengthy personal anecdotes distract from the mathematical focus
Could strengthen quantitative elements of the personal approach (e.g. explicit fit metrics)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows significant evidence of independent thinking, a clear personal approach and creativity in fitting functions and validating with water (Personal Engagement band 2). It does not reach the highest band due to reliance on standard techniques and occasional digressions from the core investigation.

strengths: Meaningful critique of derivative discontinuities between segments and error sources
Reflections link to understanding integration limits, rounding choices and photographic distortion
weaknesses: Analysis is sporadic and lacks sustained depth
Reflections are not fully developed into deep critical analysis
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student offers meaningful reflection on methodological choices and error analysis, showing clear links to their understanding (Reflection band 2). However, the critique remains intermittent and does not achieve the depth of analysis expected for the top band.

strengths: Effective application of HL-level mathematics (piecewise modelling, definite integration, trigonometric identities)
Correct setup of volume-of-revolution integrals and thoughtful piecewise function selection
weaknesses: Key algebraic steps are omitted or passed to CAS, reducing rigor
Notation slips (informal wall shift description) and minor computational imprecisions occur
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics is relevant and at the appropriate course level, with generally correct application of integrals and function transformations (Use of Mathematics band 3). Some omissions of working, reliance on CAS for main integrals and minor notation errors prevent a higher score.

strandSelections.reasoning: Selecting a family vase, defining personal aims, and deciding to test accuracy with real water show significant independent thinking, though the mathematical pathway (standard volume-of-revolution) is common.

The student shows good understanding: explains volume-of-revolution theory, justifies symmetry, and discusses difficulties with squaring non-elementary functions. However, reliance on CAS for a main integral and limited discussion of continuity smoothness indicate understanding is strong in parts, not thorough or rigorous.

Integrals of polynomials and trig expressions are mostly correct, and the final numerical check is plausible (â3 % error). Still, some algebraic steps are omitted or imprecise, and minor notation slips suggest âmostly correctâ rather than âminor errorsâ.

Most material is relevant to modelling the vase, yet several long digressions (such as multi-line algebra that later is replaced by a CAS result) and repeated explanations of the volume-of-revolution formula reduce conciseness. The piece remains mostly pertinent but could be trimmed considerably.

Appropriate symbols (Ï, â, definite integrals) and function notation are used consistently. A few slips â writing âInâ for ln, missing differential in one integral â prevent full accuracy, but overall language is mostly appropriate.

Explanations are generally clear; the reader can reconstruct each step. A few passages become dense or inconsistent (e.g. sudden switch to fraction notation without explanation), but communication is overall clear and mostly consistent.

The mathematics (piecewise modelling, definite integration, change-of-base, trig identities) is squarely at HL course level and applied effectively to solve the problem. While not sophisticated or novel enough for the top two bands, it exceeds mere relevance.

The student employs several representations: labelled photographs on a Cartesian grid, Desmos screenshots with colour-coded segments, algebraic manipulations and a comparison table for parameters. These effectively support understanding, though no numeric table of results or graph of accumulated volume is provided. This fits âeffective useâ rather than âsophisticatedâ.

The student reflects on percentage error, rounding decisions and photographic distortion, demonstrating meaningful reflection throughout. The comments are relevant but not deeply developed to reach âsubstantial critical reflectionâ.

Creativity is seen in combining diverse elementary functions to match the outline and in devising a practical water-test validation. Nevertheless, the mathematical techniques themselves remain standard, so the evidence is âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ.

The student structures the exploration in a clear sequence â introduction, aims, method, modelling, calculation, conclusion â and each section generally follows from the previous one. Transitions are present, so the reader can follow the logical steps from placing the photograph to deriving functions and then integrating. Occasional jumps (e.g. suddenly omitting algebraic working for V1) prevent it from reaching the âexcellentâ descriptor, but overall coherence is solid.

Some critique is present â e.g. recognising breaks in derivatives between segments and the inefficiency of manual integration for f1 â indicating meaningful analysis. However, analysis is sporadic and rarely probes the mathematical validity in depth.

The reflections link directly to the studentâs understanding of integration limits, function choice and error sources, showing a clear connection to learning, though not strong enough to claim an âenhancedâ understanding band.

Headings and sub-headings give a recognisable introduction-body-conclusion structure; tables and figures are referenced, and the order of functions (f1 â¦ f7) is easy to trace. Minor organisational weaknesses (e.g. citations mixed into text, some figures far from commentary) keep the work in the âwell organisedâ rather than âexceptionalâ band.

The campaign to fit functions by âtrial-and-errorâ in Desmos and colour-code segments reflects a clear personal approach directing the exploration, but it does not dominate or innovate enough for the highest band.

annotations.comment: The function label âFigure 4â conflicts with the caption numbering âFigure 5,â which may confuse readers. Ensure consistent figure numbering.
The Desmos screenshot for the sinusoidal fit effectively illustrates the model, aiding comprehension through visual representation.
The cubic polynomial in Figure 6 accurately captures mid-section curvature; consider discussing any residual misfit quantitatively.
The background description is engaging but overly detailed and verbose. Focus on essential information to enhance conciseness and relevance.
The aims are clearly stated and directly align with the explorationâs objectives, providing a strong framework for the investigation.
The method heading is generic and could be expanded to outline specific steps taken in the photo calibration and function fitting processes.
Citation style is inconsistent (e.g., âPMTâ vs âBrilliantâ). Standardize references and provide complete bibliographic details.
The water-test validation demonstrates initiative in connecting theory to practice and enriches personal engagement.
The introduction provides personal context but includes lengthy family anecdotes that distract from the mathematical focus. Consider condensing to emphasize the investigationâs rationale.
Describing the 0.2âcm wall shift informally makes the mathematical operation unclear. Introduce a formal shifted function notation.
Figure 1 is visually clear but placed far from its explanation. Move or reference it closer to the descriptive text for better coherence.
Figure 8âs cubic downward-opening model aligns well with the vaseâs narrowing, demonstrating effective function selection.
The instructions on drawing segments are repetitive. Streamline to focus on key modelling steps and avoid redundancy.
Table 1 is informative, but the header could explicitly include units (e.g., âHeight (cm)â). This improves clarity of mathematical communication.
The integral formula omits the differential term in one instance (e.g., missing âdxâ). Ensure complete notation: $$V=\pi\int_a^b(f(x))^2\,dx$$.
The final sentence trails off mid-thought (âalwaysâ). Complete the explanation to maintain clarity in the introduction of the formula.
The discussion on avoiding inverse and hyperbolic functions is insightful. Reflect further on how this choice impacts the modelâs accuracy.
The formulation $$V=\pi\int_0^{13.5}(f(x))^2dx$$ correctly sets up the volume calculation for the entire vase section.
The summation notation is effective, but explicitly state the value of \(n\) to clarify how many segments are included.
Describing the trial-and-error approach in Desmos demonstrates personal initiative, but include quantitative fit metrics (e.g., residuals) for rigor.
The detailed parameter adjustment narrative shows solid understanding of function transformations and enhances the modelling process.
Figure 2âs caption omits the domain interval, although referenced in text. Include âinterval (0,â2.3)â in the caption for clarity.
The narrative on why amplitude was unchanged is brief; expand on how this decision preserves contour fidelity.
Figure 7âs caption restates the function without explicitly noting its domain in the caption. Including domain boundaries enhances clarity.
The high curvature at the lip may introduce error; include a brief reflection on how smoothing choices affect volume accuracy.
